title: Korean Soy Garlic Tofu Rice Bowl

subs.name: 400 g paneer, cubed — skip the cornflour coating and fry until golden on two sides; it holds its shape better
tamari for a slightly less salty soy flavour, same quantity
chopped roasted peanuts for a similar nutty crunch — omit sesame allergen if substituting

steps.text: Press the tofu block between two plates weighted with something heavy for 10 minutes to push out moisture. Cut into 2 cm cubes and toss in cornflour until lightly coated — this layer gives them a golden crust when fried.
Cook rice with 2.5 cups water: 2 whistles in a pressure cooker or 18 minutes covered in a saucepan over low heat. Fluff and keep warm.

Heat oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Fry the tofu cubes in a single layer, turning every 2 minutes, until all sides are golden and crisp — about 8–10 minutes total. Transfer to a plate lined with paper. Do not crowd the pan or the tofu will steam rather than fry.

In the same kadai, reduce heat to medium. Add a teaspoon more oil if the pan is dry, then fry the minced garlic, grated ginger and sliced green chilli for 45 seconds until golden and fragrant — watch carefully so the garlic does not darken or it will taste bitter.

Add soy sauce, sugar and 2 tbsp water. Stir and bring to a bubbling simmer for 1 minute until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy. Return the fried tofu and toss gently so each piece is coated without breaking.

Drizzle gingelly oil over the glazed tofu, scatter sesame seeds and spring onion greens. Divide rice into bowls, spoon the tofu and all the sauce on top, and serve immediately with oi muchim or a simple cucumber salad on the side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tofu → 400 g paneer, cubed — skip the cornflour coating and fry until golden on two sides; it holds its shape better; soy-sauce → tamari for a slightly less salty soy flavour, same quantity; sesame-seeds → chopped roasted peanuts for a similar nutty crunch — omit sesame allergen if substituting
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Firm tofu is sold as 'firm tofu' or 'bean curd' in most Indian supermarkets and Korean-Chinese stores in cities. Cornflour (corn starch) is available in any Indian provision store under the same name. Use a kadai for frying — the sloping sides allow you to turn the cubes easily without breaking them. If tofu is unavailable, paneer makes an excellent substitute; skip the cornflour coating as paneer holds shape without it.
